schedule.description: Brewers 5-0 recap — Harrison 11 K, sweep complete
Cabrera blister — four starters now unavailable
Boyd ahead of schedule — mound session Saturday
Peralta trade clock — 11 days to June 1
Iowa 8-game skid, Noland scare, Kepley HR

stories.title: Brewers 5-0 Sweep — Harrison Dominant, Cabrera Exits with Blister
stories.angle: Kyle Harrison (11 K, 7 IP, 2 H) was dominant in Game 3. Edward Cabrera left after 3+ innings with a blister. Cubs committed 3 errors. Nine losses in 11 games, five-game losing streak. Brewers outscored Cubs 19-5 across the series. Cubs fall to 29-21, 1.5 GB behind Brewers (29-18).

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Brewers 5, Cubs 0. The Brewers swept Wrigley — outscoring the Cubs 19-5 across three games. Harrison: 7 IP, 2 hits, 11 strikeouts.

Cabrera exits with a blister after 3 innings. Five straight losses. Cubs 1.5 games back.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Brewers 5-0 Sweep Complete — Cubs' Five-Game Slide
**Type:** NEW STORY (game 3 recap)
**Sources:** ESPN game recap, CBS Sports box score, Bleacher Nation enhanced box score, Fox6 Milwaukee
**Key facts:**
- Final score: Brewers 5, Cubs 0 [HIGH — ESPN + CBS Sports + Bleacher Nation + Fox Sports]
- Kyle Harrison starting pitcher (Brewers): 7 IP, 2 H, 1 BB, 11 K [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Harrison improved to 5-1 on the season [HIGH — derived: was 4-1 before start]
- Harrison ERA reportedly 1.77 after game [MEDIUM — single AI summary; was 2.09 before]
- Edward Cabrera (Cubs starter): exited after 3+ innings with blister on right middle finger [HIGH — AP wire in multiple outlets]
- Cabrera exited after one pitch in the 4th inning (92.5 mph fastball to Joey Ortiz) [MEDIUM — Heavy.com]
- Cubs committed 3 errors [HIGH — ESPN, CBS Sports]
- David Hamilton: 3 hits, 2 runs scored [MEDIUM — single search result]
- Jake Bauers: RBI single in 3rd, extended hitting streak to career-high 11 games [MEDIUM — single AI summary]
- Cubs: 9th loss in 11 games, 5th consecutive loss [HIGH — ESPN, CBS]
- Series score across 3 games: Brewers 19, Cubs 5 [HIGH — derived from 9-3, 5-2, 5-0]
- First Brewers sweep at Wrigley since 2021 [MEDIUM — Fox6 Milwaukee]
**Follow-up:** Next series vs Astros May 22–24; Cabrera blister timeline; Cubs' response to slide

stories.title: Cubs Fall to 1.5 GB — Division Has a New Leader
stories.angle: Bold take on the division shift. Same win total (29) but Brewers have 3 fewer losses. Series run differential (19-5) shows this wasn't competitive. Cubs need to reassert themselves starting Friday vs Astros.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Milwaukee outscored the Cubs 19-5 in three games at Wrigley. The Brewers are 29-18. The Cubs are 29-21.

This isn't a blip. The division has a new leader — and Chicago needs to start playing like they belong in first place.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:
Cubs Fall to 1.5 GB — Division Has a New Leader
**Type:** NEW STORY (bold division take)
**Sources:** Derived from established records + game 3 result
**Key facts:**
- Cubs record after sweep: 29-21 [HIGH — derived from 29-20 pre-game + 1 loss]
- Brewers record after sweep: 29-18 [HIGH — derived from 28-18 pre-series opener games 2 end + 1 win in game 3]
- Cubs 1.5 GB behind Brewers [HIGH — derived: ((21-18)-(29-29))/2 = 1.5]
- Brewers have been 14-4 over last 18 games [MEDIUM — single ESPN source]
- Series run differential: Brewers outscored Cubs 19-5 across 3 games [HIGH — derived]
**Follow-up:** Standings update after Astros series; Cardinals gap

stories.title: Cabrera Blister — Rotation Depth Hits Crisis Level
stories.angle: Cabrera's blister exit is not catastrophic alone — but stacked with Horton (2027), Steele (August), and Boyd (6 weeks), the Cubs are now running on a skeleton rotation. Next start tentatively May 26, uncertain. Makes the Peralta trade case even sharper.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Cabrera left Wednesday with a blister on his right middle finger. His next start — tentatively May 26 — is uncertain.

The Cubs now have Horton (2027), Steele (August), Boyd (six weeks), and a Cabrera question mark — all at once.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Cabrera Blister — Rotation Depth at Crisis Level
**Type:** NEW STORY (injury angle)
**Sources:** Heavy.com, AP wire (DailyGazette, kdhnews), FanSided, North Side Baseball
**Key facts:**
- Cabrera: blister on right middle finger, exited May 20 vs Brewers after 3+ innings [HIGH — AP wire multiple outlets]
- Next start tentatively May 26 — unclear if he will make it [MEDIUM — Heavy.com]
- Rotation status simultaneously: Horton out until 2027, Steele until August, Boyd 4–6 weeks, Cabrera uncertain [HIGH — established across multiple pipeline runs]
- Four starters unavailable at the same time [HIGH — derived]
**Follow-up:** Cabrera IL status; Cubs likely callup options; Peralta trade urgency

stories.title: Boyd Ahead of Schedule — Mound Session Saturday, Bullpen Next Week
stories.angle: Genuinely good news in a sea of bad. Boyd is throwing pain-free and progressing faster than the 4–6 week estimate. Mound session Saturday (May 23), bullpen session next week. Counsell quoted as saying Boyd will progress at a very good pace.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Matt Boyd is ahead of schedule. Throwing pain-free, mound session Saturday, bullpen next week.

Counsell says Boyd is progressing at a very good pace. This rotation needed good news.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Boyd Recovery Ahead of Schedule
**Type:** FOLLOW-UP (Boyd recovery arc from May 18–20)
**Sources:** Yardbarker, ChiCity Sports (both citing Counsell/team sources)
**Key facts:**
- Boyd underwent meniscus surgery (left knee) — established May 20 [HIGH]
- Boyd is throwing pain-free [MEDIUM — Yardbarker single source]
- Plans mound session Saturday (May 23) [MEDIUM — single source]
- Bullpen session expected next week [MEDIUM — single source]
- Counsell quote: Boyd doing everything "at a little bit more effortless and pain-free than expected" [MEDIUM — paraphrase from single source; not directly from primary transcript]
- Counsell: "he's going to progress at a very good pace" [MEDIUM — single source]
- Original timeline was 4–6 weeks; suggesting potential return ahead of schedule [MEDIUM — implied by "ahead of schedule" framing from sources]
**Cross-reference needed:** Verify Counsell quote from a second source before marking HIGH. Current: MEDIUM.
**Follow-up:** Mound session result (Saturday May 23); formal activation timeline

stories.title: Peralta Trade Clock — 11 Days to June 1
stories.angle: Mets' June 1 decision window now 11 days out. Cabrera blister makes the urgency fresh today. Cubs have discussed Peralta with Mets; no formal deal yet. 3.12 ERA, pending free agent, Counsell connection from Milwaukee. The rotation cannot wait for Boyd to return.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Freddy Peralta. 3.12 ERA. Pending free agent. Counsell knows him from Milwaukee.

The Mets' June 1 decision point is 11 days out. The Cubs' rotation just lost another arm to a blister. The case for this trade has never been stronger.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:

**Type:** FOLLOW-UP (ongoing arc since May 14; last covered May 20)
**Sources:** MLB Trade Rumors, Yahoo Sports, Yardbarker, FanSided, ClutchPoints, BVM Sports
**Key facts:**
- Mets' June 1 decision point: either make the race or move Peralta [HIGH — multiple outlets]
- June 1 is 11 days from May 21 [HIGH — arithmetic]
- Cubs have contacted Mets about Peralta availability [HIGH — multiple outlets including MLB Trade Rumors]
- No formal deal structured yet [MEDIUM — MLB Trade Rumors caveat]
- Peralta ERA: 3.12 [HIGH — established across multiple prior pipeline runs]
- Craig Counsell managed Peralta in Milwaukee for multiple seasons [HIGH — established]

**Follow-up:** Mets' June 1 decision; any trade news

stories.title: Iowa Cubs Drop 8th Straight — Noland Ankle Scare, Kepley Homers
stories.angle: Iowa on an 8-game losing streak after losing 6-3 to Memphis. Connor Noland carted off after line drive hit his ankle — status unknown. Kane Kepley's 3-run HR in the seventh is the lone bright spot. Off-day prospect coverage.

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Iowa dropped their eighth straight game Wednesday, 6-3 to Memphis. Connor Noland was carted off after a comebacker hit his ankle — status unknown.

Kane Kepley homered in the seventh for the lone bright spot in a rough stretch.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball
